          row [e456] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e457] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0044 Cyber-safe Mode provides a defensive response to technique DE-0003.11 Watchdog Timer (WDT) for Evasion. The technique involves threat actors manipulating watchdog timer timeout values 'which could enable processes to run without interference - potentially depleting on-board resources.' The countermeasure addresses this by enabling the spacecraft to 'enter a cyber-safe mode when conditions that threaten the platform are detected' where 'all nonessential systems are shut down' to conserve resources and maintain operational integrity." [e458] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0003.11->CM0044 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e459] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "DE-0003.11->CM0044" [e460] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "create-qras • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e461] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e462] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0003.11 Technique Name: Watchdog Timer (WDT) for Evasion Technique Description: Threat actors may manipulate the WDT for several reasons including the manipulation of timeout values which could enable processes to run without interference - potentially depleting on-board resources. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0044 Countermeasure Name: Cyber-safe Mode Countermeasure Description: Provide the capability to enter the spacecraft into a configuration-controlled and integrity-protected state representing a known, operational cyber-safe state (e.g., cyber-safe mode). Spacecraft should enter a cyber-safe mode when conditions that threaten the platform are detected. Cyber-safe mode is an operating mode of a spacecraft during which all nonessential systems are shut down and the spacecraft is placed in a known good state using validated software and configuration settings. Within cyber-safe mode, authentication and encryption should still be enabled. The spacecraft should be capable of reconstituting firmware and software functions to pre-attack levels to allow for the recovery of functional capabilities. This can be performed by self-healing, or the healing can be aided from the ground. However, the spacecraft needs to have the capability to replan, based on equipment still available after a cyber-attack. The goal is for the spacecraft to resume full mission operations. If not possible, a reduced level of mission capability should be achieved. Cyber-safe mode software/configuration should be stored onboard the spacecraft in memory with hardware-based controls and should not be modifiable. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0044 Cyber-safe Mode in technique DE-0003.11 Watchdog Timer (WDT) for Evasion countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." [e463] [cursor=pointer]

The countermeasure addresses this by enabling the spacecraft to 'enter a cyber-safe mode when conditions that threaten the platform are detected' where 'all nonessential systems are shut down' to conserve resources and maintain operational integrity." [e464] [cursor=pointer]

              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0003.11 Technique Name: Watchdog Timer (WDT) for Evasion Technique Description: Threat actors may manipulate the WDT for several reasons including the manipulation of timeout values which could enable processes to run without interference - potentially depleting on-board resources. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0042 Countermeasure Name: Robust Fault Management Countermeasure Description: Ensure fault management system cannot be used against the spacecraft. Examples include: safe mode with crypto bypass, orbit correction maneuvers, affecting integrity of telemetry to cause action from ground, or some sort of proximity operation to cause spacecraft to go into safe mode. Understanding the safing procedures and ensuring they do not put the spacecraft in a more vulnerable state is key to building a resilient spacecraft. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0042 Robust Fault Management in technique DE-0003.11 Watchdog Timer (WDT) for Evasion countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs."           row [e483] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e484] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0069 Process White Listing is relevant to technique DE-0003.09 System Clock for Evasion because adversaries may attempt to alter system clocks through unauthorized processes. The technique states that 'an adversary conducting a cyber attack may be interested in altering the system clock for a variety of reasons, including misrepresentation of when certain actions took place.' The countermeasure addresses this by implementing 'process ID whitelisting on the firmware level [to] impede attackers from instigating unnecessary processes which could impact the spacecraft.'" [e485] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0003.09->CM0069 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e486] [cursor=pointer]

            cell [e489] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0003.09 Technique Name: System Clock for Evasion Technique Description: Telemetry frames are a snapshot of satellite data at a particular time. Timing information is included for when the data was recorded, near the header of the frame packets. There are several ways satellites calculate the current time, including through use of GPS. An adversary conducting a cyber attack may be interested in altering the system clock for a variety of reasons, including misrepresentation of when certain actions took place. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0069 Countermeasure Name: Process White Listing Countermeasure Description: Simple process ID whitelisting on the firmware level could impede attackers from instigating unnecessary processes which could impact the spacecraft ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0069 Process White Listing in technique DE-0003.09 System Clock for Evasion countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." [e490] [cursor=pointer]
            cell "Countermeasure CM0069 Process White Listing is relevant to technique DE-0003.09 System Clock for Evasion because adversaries may attempt to alter system clocks through unauthorized processes. The technique states that 'an adversary conducting a cyber attack may be interested in altering the system clock for a variety of reasons, including misrepresentation of when certain actions took place.' The countermeasure addresses this by implementing 'process ID whitelisting on the firmware level [to] impede attackers from instigating unnecessary processes which could impact the spacecraft.'" [e491] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Countermeasure CM0069 Process White Listing is relevant to technique DE-0003.09 System Clock for Eva..." [e492] [cursor=pointer]
            cell "Complete: grounded response accepted" [e493] [cursor=pointer]

            cell "39558ms" [e495] [cursor=pointer]
            cell "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1-TEE" [e496] [cursor=pointer]
          row [e497] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e498] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: SPARTA technique DE-0003.10 GPS Ephemeris describes how hostile actors could spoof GPS signals to cause erroneous position calculations of a satellite. SPARTA countermeasure CM0044 Cyber-safe Mode provides the capability to enter a known, operational cyber-safe state when conditions that threaten the platform are detected, such as GPS spoofing attempts. The spacecraft enters this protected mode with all nonessential systems shut down and uses validated software and configuration settings to maintain security." [e499] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0003.10->CM0044 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e500] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "DE-0003.10->CM0044" [e501] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "create-qras • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e502] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e503] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0003.10 Technique Name: GPS Ephemeris Technique Description: A satellite with a GPS receiver can use ephemeris data from GPS satellites to estimate its own position in space. A hostile actor could spoof the GPS signals to cause erroneous calculations of the satellite’s position. The received ephemeris data is often telemetered and can be monitored for indications of GPS spoofing. Reception of ephemeris data that changes suddenly without a reasonable explanation (such as a known GPS satellite handoff), could provide an indication of GPS spoofing and warrant further analysis. Threat actors could also change the course of the vehicle and falsify the telemetered data to temporarily convince ground operators the vehicle is still on a proper course. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0044 Countermeasure Name: Cyber-safe Mode Countermeasure Description: Provide the capability to enter the spacecraft into a configuration-controlled and integrity-protected state representing a known, operational cyber-safe state (e.g., cyber-safe mode). Spacecraft should enter a cyber-safe mode when conditions that threaten the platform are detected. Cyber-safe mode is an operating mode of a spacecraft during which all nonessential systems are shut down and the spacecraft is placed in a known good state using validated software and configuration settings. Within cyber-safe mode, authentication and encryption should still be enabled. The spacecraft should be capable of reconstituting firmware and software functions to pre-attack levels to allow for the recovery of functional capabilities. This can be performed by self-healing, or the healing can be aided from the ground. However, the spacecraft needs to have the capability to replan, based on equipment still available after a cyber-attack. The goal is for the spacecraft to resume full mission operations. If not possible, a reduced level of mission capability should be achieved. Cyber-safe mode software/configuration should be stored onboard the spacecraft in memory with hardware-based controls and should not be modifiable. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0044 Cyber-safe Mode in technique DE-0003.10 GPS Ephemeris countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." The spacecraft enters this protected mode with all nonessential systems shut down and uses validated software and configuration settings to maintain security." [e505] [cursor=pointer]

            cell [e538] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: Technique DE-0003.09 System Clock for Evasion involves adversaries altering satellite system clocks to misrepresent when actions occurred, which is an evasion technique. Countermeasure CM0032 On-board Intrusion Detection & Prevention is designed to monitor mission critical systems and respond to threats including evasion, providing capabilities to detect and prevent such clock manipulation attacks to ensure vehicle safety and continued operations." [e539] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0003.09->CM0032 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e540] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "DE-0003.09->CM0032" [e541] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "create-qras • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e542] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e543] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0003.09 Technique Name: System Clock for Evasion Technique Description: Telemetry frames are a snapshot of satellite data at a particular time. Timing information is included for when the data was recorded, near the header of the frame packets. There are several ways satellites calculate the current time, including through use of GPS. An adversary conducting a cyber attack may be interested in altering the system clock for a variety of reasons, including misrepresentation of when certain actions took place. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0032 Countermeasure Name: On-board Intrusion Detection & Prevention Countermeasure Description: Utilize on-board intrusion detection/prevention system that monitors the mission critical components or systems and audit/logs actions. The IDS/IPS should have the capability to respond to threats (initial access, execution, persistence, evasion, exfiltration, etc.) and it should address signature-based attacks along with dynamic never-before seen attacks using machine learning/adaptive technologies. The IDS/IPS must integrate with traditional fault management to provide a wholistic approach to faults on-board the spacecraft. Spacecraft should select and execute safe countermeasures against cyber-attacks. These countermeasures are a ready supply of options to triage against the specific types of attack and mission priorities. Minimally, the response should ensure vehicle safety and continued operations. Ideally, the goal is to trap the threat, convince the threat that it is successful, and trace and track the attacker — with or without ground support. This would support successful attribution and evolving countermeasures to mitigate the threat in the future. “Safe countermeasures” are those that are compatible with the system’s fault management system to avoid unintended effects or fratricide on the system. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0032 On-board Intrusion Detection & Prevention in technique DE-0003.09 System Clock for Evasion countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs."
